sn1.36:1.1: On one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn1.36:1.2: Then many devatās of the Satullapa host, in the far extreme of the night, their extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, stood to one side.
sn1.36:1.3: As they were standing there, one devatā recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn1.36:2.2: When you don’t take a stand in lack of conviction,
sn1.36:2.3: prestige & honor come to you from that.
sn1.36:2.4: You go to heaven on abandoning the body.
sn1.36:4.1: You should discard anger, abandon conceit,
sn1.36:4.3: When you’re not adhering to name & form,
sn1.36:4.4: you have nothing— no bonds attack you.
sn1.36:5.3: while one who is wise protects heedfulness
sn1.36:6.2: or to intimacy with sensual delight—
sn1.36:6.3: for a heedful person, absorbed in jhāna,
